name: TACTIVE-U: An Umbrella Study to Investigate the Safety and Antitumor Activity of ARV-471 in Combination With Other Medicines in Adults With ER+ Advanced or Metastatic Breast Cancer, Sub-study B (ARV-471 and Ribociclib)

titles.text: TACTIVE-U: An Umbrella Study to Investigate the Safety and Antitumor Activity of ARV-471 in Combination With Other Medicines in Adults With ER+ Advanced or Metastatic Breast Cancer, Sub-study B (ARV-471 and Ribociclib)

TACTIVE-U: AN INTERVENTIONAL SAFETY AND EFFICACY PHASE 1B/2, OPEN-LABEL UMBRELLA STUDY TO INVESTIGATE TOLERABILITY, PK, AND ANTITUMOR ACTIVITY OF VEPDEGESTRANT (ARV-471/PF-07850327), AN ORAL PROTEOLYSIS TARGETING CHIMERA, IN COMBINATION WITH OTHER ANTICANCER TREATMENTS IN PARTICIPANTS AGED 18 YEARS AND OVER WITH ER+ ADVANCED OR METASTATIC BREAST CANCER, SUB-STUDY B (ARV-471 IN COMBINATION WITH RIBOCICLIB)

amendments.name: Amendment 4

changes.name: 4 — Section 1.1; Section 1.2; Section 3.2; Section 3.3; Section 4.2; Section 9
changes.summary: Added rationale for Protocol Amendment 4 - clarified Phase 1b enrollment of this study has been permanently closed and no participants will be enrolled in, DDI cohort and Phase 2.
changes.rationale: Phase 1b enrollment has been permanently closed due to a Sponsor decision and not for safety, efficacy reasons or requests from Regulatory Authorities. No additional participants will be enrolled in any Phase.

changedSections.sectionNumber: Section 1.1; Section 1.2; Section 3.2; Section 3.3; Section 4.2; Section 9

changes.name: 4 — Risk Assessment
changes.summary: 'Developmental and reproductive toxicities' has been added as an additional potential risk of clinical significance.
changes.rationale: Risk entered in alignment with 2023 IB and following ones

changes.name: 4 — Risk Assessment
changes.summary: Summary of Data for the 'QTc Prolongation' potential risk has been modified.
changes.rationale: Summary of Data and Rationale for Risk have been updated in alignment with 2025 IB

changes.name: 4 — Section 1.1; Section 1.3; Section 4.1.2; Section 4.4; Section 6.7
changes.summary: Updated text to indicate that participants benefiting from the treatment (defined as complete/partial response or stable disease by RECIST v1.1) at the time of implementing Amendment 4 will continue treatment with study intervention per the SoA as part of the study until treatment discontinuation.
changes.rationale: To fulfill the obligation to provide study treatment to participants currently on treatment. still receiving a benefit from the study intervention

changes.name: 4 — Schedule of Activities / Study Assessments and Procedures
changes.summary: Updated SoA and added a statement that it will be reduced after implementation of Amendment 4: concomitant medications collection in eCRF no longer required; PE / vital signs / blood samples collection/ ECG performed as per clinical standard of care at the discretion of the investigator
changes.rationale: To reduce current requirements and allow several procedures to be performed as per local practice.

changedSections.sectionTitle: Schedule of Activities / Study Assessments and Procedures
changedSections.sectionNumber: Section 1.3; Section 6.9; Section 8; Section 8.2; Section 8.3.1; Section 8.3.2; Section 8.3.3; Section 8.3.4

changes.name: 4 — Treatment Continuation Beyond Disease Progression by Imaging
changes.summary: An option to continue treatment beyond progression has been removed
changes.rationale: There are no current data to support continuing study treatment beyond progression

changedSections.sectionTitle: Treatment Continuation Beyond Disease Progression by Imaging

changes.name: 4 — Synopsis
changes.summary: Reduced content in synopsis.
changes.rationale: To comply with new EU Clinical Trial Regulation

changes.name: 4 — Male/Female Participant Reproductive Inclusion Criteria
changes.summary: Contraception period edited from '30 days' to '21 days'.
changes.rationale: To be consistent with new data reported into the IB 2025 regarding the timing after the last dose of ARV-471 for required use of highly effective contraceptives. Being a combination, the most conservative timing has been used.

changes.name: 4 — Protocol Amendment History / Schedule of Activities
changes.summary: Previous SoA tables (from Amendment 3) moved to Section 10.14 as no longer applicable upon implementation of Protocol Amendment 4.
changes.rationale: Previous SoA tables moved to Appendix for reference purposes. To prevent sites from incorrectly treating per past SoA tables vs. current SoA table.

changedSections.sectionTitle: Protocol Amendment History / Schedule of Activities

changes.name: 4 — Pharmacokinetics / Biomarkers and Pharmacodynamics / Clinical Laboratory Tests
changes.summary: Sample for Biomarkers and Pharmacodynamics research detailed under this section is no longer required: These changes incorporate the PACL dated 17 Sep 2025
changes.rationale: To reduce sample collection at End of Treatment

changedSections.sectionTitle: Pharmacokinetics / Biomarkers and Pharmacodynamics / Clinical Laboratory Tests

changes.name: 4 — Adverse Events of Special Interest
changes.summary: Updated the term (AESI for ARV-471) venous embolism to venous thromboembolism (VTE).
changes.rationale: To consistently use the term VTE

changes.name: 4 — Section 1.1; Section 9.2; Section 8.4; Section 10.1.3
changes.summary: Delete text 'or their legally authorized representative's'.
changes.rationale: To comply with sponsor protocol template guidelines. The inclusion of incapacitated adults individuals for whom a legally authorized representative [LAR] may provide consent is not an option in this study.

amendments.summary: The main purpose of the amendment is to reduce the Schedule of Activities with clinical assessments per standard of care or clinical judgment of the treating physician. These changes reflect a sponsor decision to close Phase 1b enrollment and not to proceed with the DDI and Phase 2 parts and are not being implemented due to safety, efficacy, or regulatory concerns. In addition, this amendment is to update the end of study definition and the section regarding continued access to study intervention after the end of the study.

primaryReason.otherReason: The main purpose of the amendment is to reduce the Schedule of Activities with clinical assessments per standard of care or clinical judgment of the treating physician. These changes reflect a sponsor decision to close Phase 1b enrollment and not to proceed with the DDI and Phase 2 parts and are not being implemented due to safety, efficacy, or regulatory concerns. In addition, this amendment is to update the end of study definition and the section regarding continued access to study intervention after the end of the study.

amendments.name: Amendment 3

changes.name: 3 — Synopsis; Scientific Rationale for Study Design
changes.summary: DDI cohort(s) added to investigate the effect of ARV-471 on the exposure of ribociclib, and the investigation of up to 2 dose levels for ribociclib is introduced as an option.
changes.rationale: To assess the impact on ribociclib PK and add flexibility in ribociclib starting dose

changedSections.sectionTitle: Synopsis; Scientific Rationale for Study Design

changes.name: 3 — Objectives, Endpoints, and Estimands; Pharmacokinetic Analysis
changes.summary: Added objectives, endpoints, and estimands related to the new DDI cohort(s) portion of the study to evaluate the effect of ARV-471 on ribociclib PK. The sub- section numbering has been updated.
changes.rationale: To align with the new study design and the introduction of the DDI cohort(s) portion of the study.

changedSections.sectionTitle: Objectives, Endpoints, and Estimands; Pharmacokinetic Analysis

changes.name: 3 — Schedule of Activities
changes.summary: Added SoA related to the new DDI cohort(s) portion of the study, now as Table 2 and Table 5, numbers of the previous tables have been updated.
changes.rationale: To align with the new study design and the introduction of the DDI cohort(s) portion of the study. For clarification and to ensure consistency.

changes.name: 3 — Criteria for Dose Finding
changes.summary: Added text to describe the additional Dose Levels; relevant Study Schema updated.
changes.rationale: To allow the investigation of additional dose levels and to determine the effect of ARV-471 on ribociclib PK.

changes.name: 3 — Other Prohibited and/or Antitumor/Anticancer or Experimental Drugs, or Procedures
changes.summary: Updated the list of prohibited inhibitors/ inducers.
changes.rationale: Moderate CYP3A inhibitors are prohibited in the DDI cohort(s) portion of the study due to the potential effect on the plasma concentration of ribociclib.

changedSections.sectionTitle: Other Prohibited and/or Antitumor/Anticancer or Experimental Drugs, or Procedures

amendments.summary: The protocol was mainly amended to introduce a DDI cohort to evaluate any impact of ARV-471 on the PK of ribociclib, and to allow the investigation of up to 2 dose levels for ribociclib.

primaryReason.otherReason: The protocol was mainly amended to introduce a DDI cohort to evaluate any impact of ARV-471 on the PK of ribociclib, and to allow the investigation of up to 2 dose levels for ribociclib.

amendments.name: Amendment 2

changes.name: 2 — Synopsis; Sub-study Design; Justification for Dose
changes.summary: Revised the study design to escalation instead of de-escalation for ARV-471. The starting dose level of ARV-471 has been changed to 100 mg QD from 200 mg QD, in combination with ribociclib at the same starting dose level of 600 mg QD.
changes.rationale: To address regulatory feedback regarding the potential overlapping effect on QTc prolongation and drug-drug interaction with the combination of ARV-471 and ribociclib.

changedSections.sectionTitle: Synopsis; Sub-study Design; Justification for Dose

changes.name: 2 — Recommended ARV-471 and Ribociclib Dose Modifications; Dose Reductions
changes.summary: Added details to Table 8 with reference to the recommended dose modifications following QTc prolongation events and hematological toxicity
changes.rationale: To address regulatory feedback

changedSections.sectionTitle: Recommended ARV-471 and Ribociclib Dose Modifications; Dose Reductions

changes.name: 2 — Criteria for Dose Finding; Maximum Tolerated Dose
changes.summary: The target pT of 0.3 is maintained, but the target toxicity interval is adjusted to have a maximum upper bound of 0.333 instead of 0.35.
changes.rationale: To address regulatory feedback

changedSections.sectionTitle: Criteria for Dose Finding; Maximum Tolerated Dose

amendments.summary: The protocol was mainly amended to incorporate regulatory feedback.

primaryReason.otherReason: The protocol was mainly amended to incorporate regulatory feedback.

amendments.name: Amendment 1

changes.name: 1 — Schedule of Activities; Risk Assessment; Overall Benefit/Risk conclusion
changes.summary: Updated ECG monitoring and QTc evaluation:  Added one additional visit at Cycle Day 8 to assess ECG and PK  Added a triplicate ECG at Cycle 4 Day 1
changes.rationale: To address regulatory feedback, and to align with updated IB by assessing the potential time dependent impact of ARV- 471 on the QTc interval

changedSections.sectionTitle: Schedule of Activities; Risk Assessment; Overall Benefit/Risk conclusion

changes.name: 1 — Hematological DLTs
changes.summary: Added the following Hematological DLTs:  Neutropenic fever  Grade ≥3 thrombocytopenia with bleeding  Grade 4 neutropenia or thrombocytopenia >7 days
changes.rationale: To address regulatory feedback

amendments.summary: The protocol was mainly amended to incorporate regulatory feedback.

primaryReason.otherReason: The protocol was mainly amended to incorporate regulatory feedback.

versions.rationale: To investigate tolerability, pharmacokinetics (PK), and antitumor activity of vepdegestrant (ARV-471/PF-07850327) in combination with other anticancer treatments in adults with ER+ advanced or metastatic breast cancer.

population.name: Main Population
population.label: Participants must be patients with ER+/HER2- A/MBC, who have been previously treated with up to 2 lines of prior therapies for advanced/metastatic disease including 1 line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor-based regimen. The current A/MBC disease must have at least 1 measurable lesion as per RECIST v1.1.

notes.text: includesHealthySubjects inferred as false because the protocol population is disease-specific (oncology).

cohorts.name: 1 (Starting dose)
cohorts.label: ARV-471 100 mg QD; Ribociclib 600 mg QD
cohorts.description: ARV-471 100 mg QD; Ribociclib 600 mg QD

cohorts.label: ARV-471 200 mg QD; Ribociclib 600 mg QD
cohorts.description: ARV-471 200 mg QD; Ribociclib 600 mg QD

cohorts.label: ARV-471 100 mg QD; Ribociclib 400 mg QD
cohorts.description: ARV-471 100 mg QD; Ribociclib 400 mg QD

cohorts.name: -1A
cohorts.label: ARV-471 200 mg QD; Ribociclib 400 mg QD
cohorts.description: ARV-471 200 mg QD; Ribociclib 400 mg QD

cohorts.name: DDI assessment cohort(s)
cohorts.label: DDI assessment cohort(s)
cohorts.description: Based on the doses selected as RP2D(s) for Phase 2, there will be one or more DDI assessment cohort(s) at the end of Phase 1b, with approximately 6-12 participants per cohort.

population.description: Participants must be patients with ER+/HER2- A/MBC, who have been previously treated with up to 2 lines of prior therapies for advanced/metastatic disease including 1 line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor-based regimen. The current A/MBC disease must have at least 1 measurable lesion as per RECIST v1.1.

arms.name: ARV-471 and ribociclib
arms.label: ARV-471 and ribociclib
arms.description: The starting dose level of ARV-471 will be 100 mg QD, administered continuously on 28-day cycles and with food. The starting dose of ribociclib will be 600 mg QD consecutively for 21 days followed by 7 days off treatment on 28-day cycles with or without food.

studyDesigns.name: Prospective, open-label, multicenter, Phase 1b/2 sub-study
studyDesigns.label: C4891023 (Sub-Study B: ARV-471 + Ribociclib)

epochs.name: Screening
epochs.label: Screening
epochs.description: The screening period will occur within 28 days prior to first dose. Study Day: ≤ 28 days prior to first dose. Informed consent must be obtained within 28 days of enrollment and to any protocol-required assessments prior being performed (Section 10.1.3).

epochs.name: ARV-471 Lead-In Period
epochs.label: ARV-471 Lead-In Period
epochs.description: A 7-day PK lead-in period in which ARV-471 is administered alone will be included in Phase 1b to determine the effect of ribociclib on ARV-471 PK. The PK lead-in will be completed only for cohorts with the highest dose level of ribociclib (600 mg). If multiple doses of ARV-471 are evaluated with 600 mg ribociclib, they will be combined to assess the DDI impact of ribociclib on ARV-471. Study Day: Day -7; Study Day: Day -1.

epochs.name: Combination Treatment Phase 1 Cycle = 28 days
epochs.label: Combination Treatment Phase 1 Cycle = 28 days
epochs.description: Study intervention will be administered in 28-day cycles. Visit on Day 1 of every cycle (1 cycle = 28 days). The starting dose level of ARV-471 will be 100 mg QD, administered continuously on 28-day cycles and with food. The starting dose of ribociclib will be 600 mg QD consecutively for 21 days followed by 7 days off treatment on 28-day cycles with or without food. Phase 1b will evaluate the safety and tolerability of ARV-471 in combination with ribociclib and select a recommended dose and regimen for the combination (combination RP2D) via the mTPI-2 dose-finding design.

epochs.name: EOT/ Withdrawal
epochs.label: EOT/ Withdrawal
epochs.description: End of Treatment/Withdrawal: As soon as study intervention is permanently discontinued and no more than 28 calendar days after last dose of oral study intervention. End of Treatment/Withdrawal: These assessments are to be performed only if they have not been completed during the previous 28 days on study, or within the previous 8 weeks for disease assessments if EOT occurred during the first year of treatment, or within the previous 12 weeks if EOT visit occurred after the first year of treatment, or within the previous 24 weeks for bone scans, unless needed for monitoring of safety findings.

epochs.name: Post-Treatment Follow-Up
epochs.label: Post-Treatment Follow-Up
epochs.description: Post Treatment Follow-up: Follow up visit will be performed at least 28 days (+7 days) from last dose of study intervention OR prior to the initiation of any new anticancer therapy whichever comes first. The SAE reporting period is 28 days from last dose regardless of the initiation of a new anticancer therapy (see Section 8.4.1). At least 28 calendar days (+7 days) after discontinuation of study intervention or prior if a new anticancer therapy is initiated (whichever comes first), participants will return to site to undergo the safety follow-up assessments as reported in the SoA.

elements.name: EL4
elements.label: Study intervention administration
elements.description: Study intervention will be administered in 28-day cycles. Visit on Day 1 of every cycle (1 cycle = 28 days). The starting dose level of ARV-471 will be 100 mg QD, administered continuously on 28-day cycles and with food. The starting dose of ribociclib will be 600 mg QD consecutively for 21 days followed by 7 days off treatment on 28-day cycles with or without food.

transitionEndRule.name: Permanent discontinuation of study intervention
transitionEndRule.text: As soon as study intervention is permanently discontinued and no more than 28 calendar days after last dose of oral study intervention.

transitionStartRule.name: Cycle visit (start)
transitionStartRule.text: Visit on Day 1 of every cycle (1 cycle = 28 days)

elements.name: EL5
elements.label: Active collection period for AEs and SAEs
elements.description: The time period for actively eliciting and collecting AEs and SAEs ('active collection period') for each participant begins from the time the participant provides informed consent, which is obtained before undergoing any study-related procedure and/or receiving study intervention), through and including a minimum of 28 calendar days, except as indicated below, after the last administration of the study intervention.

transitionEndRule.name: Active collection period end
transitionEndRule.text: Through and including a minimum of 28 calendar days after the last administration of the study intervention

transitionStartRule.name: Active collection period start
transitionStartRule.text: From the time the participant provides informed consent

elements.name: EL6
elements.label: Post-Treatment Follow-up Visit
elements.description: Post Treatment Follow-up: Follow up visit will be performed at least 28 days (+7 days) from last dose of study intervention OR prior to the initiation of any new anticancer therapy whichever comes first. The SAE reporting period is 28 days from last dose regardless of the initiation of a new anticancer therapy (see Section 8.4.1).

transitionEndRule.name: Post-treatment follow-up end
transitionEndRule.text: The SAE reporting period is 28 days from last dose

transitionStartRule.name: Post-treatment follow-up start
transitionStartRule.text: At least 28 calendar days (+7 days) after discontinuation of study intervention or prior if a new anticancer therapy is initiated (whichever comes first)

elements.name: EL1
elements.label: Informed Consent Process
elements.description: The investigator or qualified designee will explain the nature of the study, including the risks and benefits, to the participant and answer all questions regarding the study. Participants will be required to sign a statement of informed consent that meets the requirements of 21 CFR 50, local regulations, ICH GCP guidelines, privacy and data protection requirements, where applicable, and the IRB/EC or study center. Informed consent must be obtained within 28 days of enrollment and to any protocol-required assessments prior being performed (Section 10.1.3).

elements.name: EL2
elements.label: ARV-471 Lead-In Period
elements.description: A 7-day PK lead-in period in which ARV-471 is administered alone will be included in Phase 1b to determine the effect of ribociclib on ARV-471 PK. The PK lead-in will be completed only for cohorts with the highest dose level of ribociclib (600 mg).

elements.name: EL3
elements.label: DDI assessment cohort 7-day PK lead-in (ribociclib monotherapy)
elements.description: Participants in the DDI assessment cohort(s) will be treated with monotherapy ribociclib QD during a 7-day PK lead-in period. At the end of the 7-day PK lead-in, ARV-471 QD will then be administered in combination with ribociclib QD starting on C1D1.

transitionEndRule.name: DDI lead-in end
transitionEndRule.text: At the end of the 7-day PK lead-in, ARV-471 QD will then be administered in combination with ribociclib QD starting on C1D1

estimands.name: Primary Estimand Phase 1b
estimands.description: DLT rate estimated based on data from DLT-evaluable participants during the DLT observation period (Cycle 1).
estimands.populationSummary: DLT-evaluable participants with A/MBC who received at least one dose and either experienced a DLT during Cycle 1 or completed the DLT observation period; participants receiving <75% of planned dose for non-treatment-related reasons are non-evaluable.

estimands.name: Primary Estimand Phase 2 (ORR)
estimands.description: The treatment effect of ARV-471 in combination with ribociclib on ORR per RECIST v1.1. response criteria as determined by investigator assessment.
estimands.populationSummary: Participants with A/MBC who received at least one dose of study intervention (analysis set for response/efficacy).

intercurrentEvents.name: Subsequent anticancer therapy
intercurrentEvents.text: All data collected after an intercurrent event of subsequent anticancer therapy will be excluded.

intercurrentEvents.name: No post-baseline disease assessment / early PD / death before response
intercurrentEvents.text: Participants who do not have a post- baseline disease assessment due to early PD, who receive anticancer therapies other than the study intervention prior to achieving an objective response, or who die, experience documented PD, or stop disease assessments for any reason prior to achieving an objective response will be counted as non- responders.

studyDesigns.rationale: The combination of ribociclib with ARV-471 is expected to provide benefit to pretreated patients with ER+/HER2- A/MBC.

objectives.name: OBJ_PR_01
objectives.text: Assess safety and tolerability of ARV-471 given with ribociclib in participants with ER+/HER2- advanced/metastatic breast cancer to select a recommended Phase 2 dose (RP2D).

endpoints.name: EP_PR_01
endpoints.text: Occurrence of dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs) during the Cycle 1 DLT observation period.

objectives.description: To assess safety and tolerability of ARV-471 in combination with ribociclib in participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC, to select a RP2D.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_01
objectives.text: Evaluate the overall safety profile of ARV-471 administered in combination with ribociclib in the study population.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_01_AE
endpoints.text: Adverse events characterized by type, frequency, severity (NCI CTCAE v5.0), timing, seriousness, and relationship to study interventions.
endpoints.description: AEs as characterized by type, frequency, intensity (as graded by NCI CTCAE version 5.0), timing, seriousness, and relationship to ARV-471 in combination with ribociclib.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_02_LAB
endpoints.text: Laboratory test abnormalities characterized by type, frequency and severity (NCI CTCAE v5.0) and timing.
endpoints.description: Laboratory test abnormalities as characterized by type, frequency, intensity (as graded by NCI CTCAE version 5.0), and timing.

objectives.description: To evaluate the overall safety profile.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_02
objectives.text: Evaluate antitumor activity of ARV-471 in combination with ribociclib in participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_03_ORR
endpoints.text: Objective response rate (confirmed CR or PR) per investigator assessment using RECIST v1.1.
endpoints.description: Confirmed OR (CR or PR) by investigator assessment.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_04_DoR
endpoints.text: Duration of response (DoR) measured from first documented confirmed response to objective tumor progression or death.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_05_CBR
endpoints.text: Clinical benefit rate (CBR) defined as confirmed CR or PR at any time, or stable disease ≥24 weeks.
endpoints.description: CBR (confirmed CR or PR at any time, or SD ≥24 weeks) by investigator assessment.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_06_PFS
endpoints.text: Progression-free survival (PFS): time from first dose to first documentation of PD or death from any cause.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_07_OS
endpoints.text: Overall survival (OS): time from first dose to death due to any cause.

objectives.description: To evaluate antitumor activity of ARV-471 in combination with ribociclib.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_03_PK_ARV471
objectives.text: Evaluate the effect of ribociclib on the pharmacokinetics of ARV-471.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_08_PK_ARV471_AUC_CMAX
endpoints.text: AUCtau and Cmax of ARV-471 with and without co-administration of ribociclib.
endpoints.description: AUC tau and C max of ARV-471 with and without co- administration of ribociclib.

objectives.description: To evaluate the effect of ribociclib on PK of ARV471.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_04_PK_EXPOSURE
objectives.text: Evaluate plasma exposure of ARV-471, ARV-473, and ribociclib when administered in combination.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_09_PK_CONC
endpoints.text: Plasma concentrations of ARV-471, ARV-473, and ribociclib.
endpoints.description: Plasma concentrations of ARV- 471, ARV-473, and ribociclib.

objectives.description: To evaluate the plasma exposure of ARV-471, ARV-473 and ribociclib when ARV-471 and ribociclib are given in combination.

objectives.name: OBJ_EX_01_TUMOR_BIOLOGY
objectives.text: Explore tumor biology and potential treatment-related biomarker changes associated with ARV-471 treatment.

endpoints.name: EP_EX_01_BIOMARKER
endpoints.text: Measurements of biomarkers (DNA, RNA, protein, or cell types) from peripheral blood and/or tumor tissue to evaluate treatment-related biological effects.
endpoints.description: Measurements of biomarkers (DNA, RNA, protein, or defined cell types) resulting from analyses of peripheral blood and/or tumor tissue biospecimen.

endpoints.name: EP_EX_02_CT DNA
endpoints.text: Quantitative changes in plasma circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) from pre-treatment to on-treatment to explore associations with clinical outcomes.
endpoints.description: ctDNA plasma quantitative changes from pre-treatment to evaluate potential predictability of their associations with clinical outcomes.

objectives.description: To evaluate tumor biology and explore the treatment effect of ARV-471.

objectives.name: OBJ_PR_02_PHASE2_DDI
objectives.text: Evaluate the effect of ARV-471 on the pharmacokinetics of ribociclib (steady-state AUCtau and Cmax).

endpoints.name: EP_PR_02_PK_RIBO
endpoints.text: Steady-state AUCtau and Cmax of ribociclib with and without co-administration of ARV-471.
endpoints.description: Steady-state AUC tau and C max of ribociclib with and without co- administration of ARV- 471.

objectives.description: To evaluate the effect of ARV-471 on PK of ribociclib.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_05_PHASE2_SAFETY
objectives.text: Evaluate the overall safety profile in the Phase 2/ DDI cohorts.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_06_INC_AE_SAE
endpoints.text: Incidence of adverse events (AEs) and serious adverse events (SAEs).

endpoints.name: EP_SC_07_INC_LAB_ECG
endpoints.text: Incidence of laboratory abnormalities and ECG abnormalities.
endpoints.description: Incidence of laboratory abnormalities.  Incidence of ECG abnormalities.

objectives.description: To evaluate the overall safety profile.

indications.name: IND1
indications.label: ER+ advanced or metastatic breast cancer
indications.description: ER+ (estrogen receptor positive) advanced or metastatic breast cancer

scheduleTimelines.label: ARV-471 Lead-In Timeline
scheduleTimelines.entryCondition: Cohort with the highest dose level of ribociclib (600 mg) — ARV-471 lead-in completed

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants aged 18 years or older (or the minimum age of consent in ac
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants aged 18 years or older (or the minimum age of consent in accordance with local regulations) at screening. Refer to Appendix 4 for reproductive criteria for male (Section 10.4.1) and female (Section 10.4.2) participants.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Histological or cytological diagnosis of A/MBC that is not amendable to
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Histological or cytological diagnosis of A/MBC that is not amendable to surgical resection with curative intent (≥1% ER+ stained cells as per local practice [Allison et al, 2020] on the most recent tumor biopsy HER2- tumor by IHC or in-situ hybridization per ASCO/CAP) (Wolff 2018):

 Participants who have bilateral breast cancers that are both ER+/HER2- are eligible.

 Tumor block collected at the time of diagnosis with local recurrent or metastatic disease or archival tumor tissue is required for inclusion. Refer to Section 8.7.1.1.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants must have at least 1 measurable lesion as defined by RECIST
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants must have at least 1 measurable lesion as defined by RECIST v1.1. Participants with bone lesions only could be included if at least 1 bone lesion has a measurable component as for RECIST v1.1 (see Appendix 12).

eligibilityCriteria.name:  History of any other malignancies within the past three years, except f
eligibilityCriteria.text:  History of any other malignancies within the past three years, except for the following: (1) adequately treated basal or squamous cell carcinoma of the skin; (2) curatively treated in situ carcinoma of the cervix. All other malignancies must have been curatively treated with no evidence of disease for  3 years. Participants with inflammatory breast cancer are excluded.

eligibilityCriteria.name:  Participants with newly diagnosed brain metastases, or symptomatic C
eligibilityCriteria.text:  Participants with newly diagnosed brain metastases, or symptomatic CNS metastases, carcinomatous meningitis, or leptomeningeal disease as indicated by clinical symptoms, cerebral edema, and/or progressive growth. Participants with a history of CNS metastases or cord compression are eligible if they have been definitively treated (eg, radiotherapy, stereotactic surgery) and clinically stable (including participants with residual CNS symptoms/deficits) and discontinued anti-seizure medications and corticosteroids for at least 14 days prior to enrollment.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Any unresolved toxicities from prior surgeries or therapies Grade >1 at t
eligibilityCriteria.text: Any unresolved toxicities from prior surgeries or therapies Grade >1 at the time of study enrollment except for Grade 2 alopecia, peripheral neuropathy, arthralgia, or other toxicities not considered a safety risk for the participant per the investigator's judgment.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Prior procedures and treatment:  Major surgery, radiotherapy, prior endoc
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Prior procedures and treatment:

 Major surgery, radiotherapy, prior endocrine therapy, CDK4/6 inhibitors, or other anticancer treatments within 14 days prior to enrollment in the study (for anticancer therapy containing an antibody-based agents, approved or investigational, 28 days or 5 half-lives, whichever is shorter).

 Participation in other clinical studies involving investigational drug(s) or vaccine(s) within 28 days or 5 half-lives prior enrollment in the study (whichever is shorter). Participants may be eligible if they are in the follow-up Phase of an investigational study provided that at least 5 half-lives have elapsed from the last dose.

 Participants who received: prior radiotherapy to ≥25% of bone marrow, or prior hematopoietic stem cell, or bone marrow transplantation are not eligible independent of when it was received.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Hepatic dysfunction defined as:  Total bilirubin  1.5 × ULN unless the
13. Hematologic abnormalities defined as:  ANC  1,500/mm 3 or 1.5 × 10 9 /
Known active infection including hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, a
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Known active infection including hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, and HIV or AIDS-related illness (screening for chronic conditions is not required). A participant who has a positive test result for SARS-CoV-2 infection, is known to have asymptomatic infection, or is suspected of having SARS-CoV-2, is not eligible for the study, except in case SARS-CoV-2 infection occurring during the screening period is resolved at least 14 days prior to enrollment.

Note : active viral hepatitis infection is defined as untreated/uncontrolled hepatitis (e.g., hepatitis B patients who are treated and whose LFTs meet eligibility are not considered to have active hepatitis B).

For HIV infection, the study participant is eligible if the disease is controlled with treatment, and the therapy does not exclude the participant as per Exclusion criterion #1.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Other Inclusion criteria: . ECOG PS ≤1.
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Other Inclusion criteria:
. ECOG PS ≤1.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Prior anticancer therapies:  At least one and no more than 2 lines of prio
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Prior anticancer therapies:
 At least one and no more than 2 lines of prior therapies for advanced/metastatic disease:

o ≤1 prior chemotherapy (no antibody-drug conjugates permitted)

o ≤2 prior endocrine-based therapies

o No prior elacestrant or experimental endocrine therapy (eg, SERD, CERAN, PROTAC, SERCA) permitted.

 1, and only 1, line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor-based regimen is required (independent of the setting eg, adjuvant or advanced/metastatic). If prior CDK4/6 inhibitor was permanently discontinued due to adverse events, the participant will not be eligible. If prior CDK4/6 inhibitor required dose reductions due to an adverse event, refer to Section 6.9.1 for the eligibility evaluation.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Impaired cardiovascular function or clinically significant cardiovascula
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Impaired cardiovascular function or clinically significant cardiovascular diseases, as defined as:

 Any of the following in the previous 6 months: myocardial infarction, severe/unstable angina, coronary/peripheral artery bypass graft, symptomatic congestive heart failure (New York Heart Association class III or IV), cerebrovascular accident, transient ischemic attack, or symptomatic pulmonary embolism or other clinically significant episode of thromboembolic disease.

 Symptomatic cardiac valve disease. Participants with mitral valve prolapse that is asymptomatic or not associated with clinically significant sequalae (eg, mitral regurgitation) are eligible.

 QTcF ≥450 msec at screening or any of the following in the previous 6 months: congenital long QT syndrome, Torsade de Pointes, clinically important arrhythmias, left anterior hemiblock (bifascicular block), ongoing cardiac dysrhythmias of Grade ≥2, atrial fibrillation of any grade.

 Electrolyte abnormalities (eg hypo- or hyperkalemia, acidemia, hypo-or hypercalcemia, hypo-or hypermagnesemia).

eligibilityCriteria.name: Concurrent administration of medications, food, or herb supplements tha
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Concurrent administration of medications, food, or herb supplements that are strong inhibitors and strong inducers of CYP3A, and drugs known to predispose to Torsade de Pointes or QT interval prolongation. Prior use of strong CYP3A inhibitors must be stopped 7 days and strong inducers of CYP3A must be stopped 14 days before enrollment in the study. Refer to Section 6.9 and Appendix 9 for the list of prohibited drugs.

In addition, for participants to be enrolled in the DDI Assessment Cohort(s): moderate CYP3A inhibitors and inducers are also prohibited to avoid potential impact on DDI analysis. Refer to Sections 6.9.3 and 10.9 for the list of examples of prohibited drugs.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Investigator site staff directly involved in the conduct of the study an
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Investigator site staff directly involved in the conduct of the study and their family members *, site staff otherwise supervised by the investigator, and sponsor and sponsor delegate employees directly involved in the conduct of the study and their family members.

* 'Family members' means any one of the following legally competent persons: Spouse; parents; children (including adopted children); brothers; sisters; and spouses of brothers and sisters; and any individual related by blood or affinity whose close association with the subject is the equivalent of a family relationship.

eligibilityCriteria.name:  Participants in visceral crisis at risk of life-threatening complication
eligibilityCriteria.text:  Participants in visceral crisis at risk of life-threatening complications in the short term, including participants with massive uncontrolled effusions (pleural, pericardial, and/or peritoneal), pulmonary lymphangitis, and liver involvement  50%.

eligibilityCriteria.name:  Participants with a known history of drug-induced pneumonitis or othe
eligibilityCriteria.text:  Participants with a known history of drug-induced pneumonitis or other significant symptomatic deterioration of lung functions.

eligibilityCriteria.name:  Refractory nausea and vomiting, chronic GI disease, GI ulcer, GI bleed
eligibilityCriteria.text:  Refractory nausea and vomiting, chronic GI disease, GI ulcer, GI bleeding, inability to swallow the formulated product, or previous, significant gastric bowel resection that would preclude adequate absorption of study interventions.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Renal impairment defined by an eGFR  45 mL/min/1.73 m 2 *. In equivocal
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Renal impairment defined by an eGFR  45 mL/min/1.73 m 2 *. In equivocal cases, a 24-hour urine collection test can be used to estimate the creatinine clearance more accurately.

* Use the 2021 CKD-EPI eGFR (serum creatinine only) equation [see Appendix 7] to calculate eGFR (in units of mL/min/1.73 m²). By multiplication of {[eGFR] × [nomogram-based participant body surface area (m 2 )} = eCrCl in mL/min. The eGFR cutoff is set to 45 mL/min based on the preliminary results of ARV-471-CPhm-103 study in healthy post-menopausal female participants indicating that the renal clearance of ARV-471 is a minor pathway representing less than 0.1% of total clearance. Moreover, as per ribociclib prescribing information no dosage adjustment of ribociclib is required for participants with mild or moderate renal impairment.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Any medical or psychiatric condition including recent (within the past y
eligibilityCriteria.text: Any medical or psychiatric condition including recent (within the past year) or active suicidal ideation/behavior or laboratory abnormality that may increase the risk of study participation or, in the investigator's judgment, make the participant inappropriate for the study, or any social situations that would limit study compliance.

studyInterventions.name: Ribociclib
studyInterventions.description: CDK4/6 inhibitor administered orally in combination with ARV-471 per protocol-specified dose levels and schedule.

administrations.name: Ribociclib 600 mg QD — oral, 21 days on/7 days off (28-day cycle)

duration.text: 21 days on, 7 days off per 28-day cycle

administrations.description: Three 200 mg tablets QD for 21 consecutive days followed by 7 days off treatment on 28-day cycles; may be taken with or without food.

administrations.name: Ribociclib 400 mg QD — oral, 21 days on/7 days off (28-day cycle)

duration.text: 21 days on, 7 days off per 28-day cycle

administrations.description: Two 200 mg tablets QD for 21 consecutive days followed by 7 days off treatment on 28-day cycles; may be taken with or without food.

administrations.name: Ribociclib 200 mg QD — oral, 21 days on/7 days off (28-day cycle)

duration.text: 21 days on, 7 days off per 28-day cycle

administrations.description: One 200 mg tablet QD for 21 consecutive days followed by 7 days off treatment on 28-day cycles; may be taken with or without food.

administrableProducts.name: Ribociclib
administrableProducts.description: 200 mg tablets provided in blister packs.

substance.name: ribociclib
substance.description: Selective CDK4 and CDK6 inhibitor that blocks cell cycle progression from G1 to S phase.

studyInterventions.name: ARV-471
studyInterventions.description: Study investigational product evaluated as oral ER degrader (PROTAC).

administrations.name: ARV-471 100 mg QD — oral, continuous (28-day cycles)
administrations.description: Must be administered with food. During lead-in ARV-471 is taken alone in the morning with food; after lead-in take ARV-471 followed by ribociclib at approximately the same time (no more than 30 minutes apart) as applicable.

administrations.name: ARV-471 200 mg QD — oral, continuous (28-day cycles)
administrations.description: Must be administered with food. Dose level per protocol provisional dose levels.

administrableProducts.name: ARV-471
administrableProducts.description: 100 mg tablets provided in a bottle with child-resistant cap.

substance.name: ARV-471
substance.description: PROTAC small molecule targeting the estrogen receptor (ER) to induce proteasomal degradation of ER.
